bash_commands.command: find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg -path "*alerting*" -name "*.go" -type f | grep -v test | grep -v mock | head -50
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg/services -type d -name "*alert*" | grep -v ngalert | grep -v provisioning | head -30
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg -name "*.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "legacy.*alert\|alert.*legacy\|migrate.*alert\|alert.*migrate" | grep -v test | head -40
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg -name "*.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "migrate\|convert" | xargs grep -l "alert" | grep -v test | head -30
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg -name "*.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "dashboard.*alert\|alert.*dashboard" | grep -v test | grep -v migration | head -20
grep -r "AddDashAlertMigration\|AddDashboard.*Migration\|move dashboard alerts" /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg --include="*.go" | grep -v test | head -20
grep -r "move dashboard alerts to unified alerting" /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg --include="*.go" | head -20
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec -path "*/db/migrations*" -name "*.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "alert" | grep -v test | head -20
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg -name "*.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "AlertRuleGroup\|AlertInstance\|alert_rule" | xargs grep -l "alert\s\|from alert\|SELECT.*alert" | grep -v test | head -30
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg -name "*.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "SELECT.*alert\|FROM alert" | xargs grep -l "alert_rule\|AlertRule" | head -20
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg -name "*.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "dashboard_id.*alert\|alert.*dashboard_id" | grep -v test | head -20
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg -name "*.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "MigrateAlerts\|MigratedAlert\|ConvertAlert" | grep -v test | head -20
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg -type d -name "*legacy*" | grep -v test | grep -v mock | head -30
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg/services/ngalert -name "*.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "eval\|evaluation\|engine" | grep -v test | head -20
find /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg/services/ngalert -maxdepth 2 -name "*.go" -type f | grep -v test | grep -v mock | head -40
for dir in eval schedule sender state; do echo "=== $dir ==="; ls /workspace/grafana--26d36ec/pkg/services/ngalert/$dir/*.go 2>/dev/null | grep -v test | grep -v mock | head -5; done

    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/alert_rules.go", "symbol": "AlertRuleService"},

    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/ualert.go", "symbol": "upgradeNgAlerting.Exec"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/ualert.go", "symbol": "upgradeNgAlerting.updateAlertConfigurations"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/ngalert/ngalert.go", "symbol": "AlertNG.init"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/ngalert/ngalert.go", "symbol": "AlertNG.Run"},

    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/ngalert/eval/eval.go", "symbol": "conditionEvaluator.Evaluate"}
  ],
  "text": "The Grafana legacy alerting to unified alerting migration involves three major components:\n\n## Legacy Alert Rule Storage\nThe legacy alert system stored alert rules in a simple database table defined in `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/alert_mig.go`. The `addAlertMigrations()` function creates the legacy `alert` table with fields like dashboard_id, panel_id, name, message, settings, and state. This table represents dashboard-level alerts that were tied to specific dashboard panels. The legacy API storage layer is implemented in `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/alertrule/legacy_storage.go` through the `legacyStorage` struct, which provides REST API access to these legacy alert rules by delegating to the provisioning service.\n\n## Unified Alert Rule Storage and Engine\nThe unified alerting (ngalert) engine replaces the legacy system with a new architecture:\n\n1. **Data Model**: `pkg/services/ngalert/models/alert_rule.go` defines the `AlertRule` struct that represents the new alert rule format with enhanced features including rule groups, expressions, and flexible notification settings.\n\n2. **Storage**: `pkg/services/ngalert/store/alert_rule.go` implements the `DBstore` struct providing database operations for alert rules including `InsertAlertRules()`, `GetAlertRuleByUID()`, `UpdateAlertRules()`, and `ListAlertRules()`.\n\n3. **Main Service**: `pkg/services/ngalert/ngalert.go` contains the `AlertNG` struct which is the primary unified alerting service initialized through `ProvideService()` function. This service orchestrates all alerting components.\n\n4. **Evaluation Engine**: `pkg/services/ngalert/eval/eval.go` implements alert rule evaluation through `NewEvaluatorFactory()` and the `conditionEvaluator` struct, which evaluates alert conditions against datasources.\n\n5. **Scheduler**: `pkg/services/ngalert/schedule/schedule.go` implements the `schedule` struct created by `NewScheduler()`, which periodically evaluates alert rules and manages their execution.\n\n## Migration Script\nThe migration from legacy to unified alerting is implemented across multiple files:\n\n1. **Main Migration**: `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/ualert.go` contains the core migration logic through the `upgradeNgAlerting` struct. The `FixEarlyMigration()` function handles early ng-alerting configurations, while `AddDashboardUIDPanelIDMigration()` migrates dashboard and panel references from annotations. The `upgradeNgAlerting.Exec()` method orchestrates the migration process by updating alert configurations and moving alertmanager files.\n\n2. **Table Creation**: `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/tables.go` defines all new unified alerting tables through `AddTablesMigrations()`, including alert_rule, alert_rule_version, alert_instance, alert_configuration, and related tables.\n\n3. **Migration Service**: `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/migration_service_migration.go` moves legacy alert migration status from the migration log to kvstore.\n\n4. **GUID Migration**: `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/alert_rule_version_guid_mig.go` handles adding and managing GUID columns for alert rules and versions.\n\nThe migration process converts legacy dashboard-based alerts (stored in the `alert` table) into the new unified alert rule format. It preserves metadata through special labels and annotations like `__legacy_` prefixed labels to route alerts through appropriate notification channels and silence rules. The `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/alertrule/compat.go` provides compatibility functions like `convertToK8sResource()` to convert between internal and API formats.\n\nThe unified alerting system is integrated with provisioning services (`pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/alert_rules.go`), state management (`pkg/services/ngalert/state/manager.go`), notification sending (`pkg/services/ngalert/sender/sender.go`), and legacy notification storage (`pkg/services/ngalert/notifier/legacy_storage/compat.go`) to provide backward compatibility while offering a modern alerting platform."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Grafana Legacy Alerting to Unified Alerting Migration

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement both the legacy alerting engine and the unified alerting (ngalert) engine: the legacy alert rule storage, the unified alert rule storage, and the migration script that converts legacy rules to unified format.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all the relevant Go source files related to legacy alerting, unified alerting, and the migration script in the grafana/grafana repository. Let me start by exploring the workspace structure.
Now let me create the comprehensive answer file with all the relevant Go source files I've found:

Perfect! I've created the comprehensive answer file at `/workspace/answer.json` with my findings. Here's a summary of what I discovered:

## Summary of Findings

I've identified all the Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement the legacy alerting engine, unified alerting (ngalert) engine, and the migration script that converts legacy rules to unified format:

### **Legacy Alert Rule Storage**
- `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/alert_mig.go` - Defines the legacy alert table structure (dashboard_id, panel_id, settings, etc.)
- `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/alertrule/legacy_storage.go` - API storage layer providing REST access to legacy alerts

### **Unified Alert Rule Storage & Engine**
- `pkg/services/ngalert/models/alert_rule.go` - AlertRule data model
- `pkg/services/ngalert/store/alert_rule.go` - Database storage implementation (DBstore)
- `pkg/services/ngalert/ngalert.go` - Main AlertNG unified alerting service
- `pkg/services/ngalert/eval/eval.go` - Alert evaluation engine
- `pkg/services/ngalert/schedule/schedule.go` - Alert scheduler
- `pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/alert_rules.go` - Alert rules provisioning service

### **Migration Scripts**
- `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/ualert.go` - Core migration logic (upgradeNgAlerting struct)
- `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/tables.go` - New unified alerting table definitions
- `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/migration_service_migration.go` - Migration status tracking
- `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/alert_rule_version_guid_mig.go` - GUID migration for versions

### **Supporting Components**
- `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/alertrule/compat.go` - Compatibility/conversion layer
- `pkg/services/ngalert/state/manager.go` - State management
- `pkg/services/ngalert/sender/sender.go` - Notification sender

The answer file includes complete file paths, key symbols/structs, functional call chains, and a detailed narrative explanation of how the migration process works.

  "text": "The Grafana legacy alerting to unified alerting migration involves three major components:\n\n## Legacy Alert Rule Storage\nThe legacy alert system stored alert rules in a simple database table defined in `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/alert_mig.go`. The `addAlertMigrations()` function creates the legacy `alert` table with fields like dashboard_id, panel_id, name, message, settings, and state. This table represents dashboard-level alerts that were tied to specific dashboard panels. The legacy API storage layer is implemented in `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/alertrule/legacy_storage.go` through the `legacyStorage` struct, which provides REST API access to these legacy alert rules by delegating to the provisioning service.\n\n## Unified Alert Rule Storage and Engine\nThe unified alerting (ngalert) engine replaces the legacy system with a new architecture:\n\n1. **Data Model**: `pkg/services/ngalert/models/alert_rule.go` defines the `AlertRule` struct that represents the new alert rule format with enhanced features including rule groups, expressions, and flexible notification settings.\n\n2. **Storage**: `pkg/services/ngalert/store/alert_rule.go` implements the `DBstore` struct providing database operations for alert rules including `InsertAlertRules()`, `GetAlertRuleByUID()`, `UpdateAlertRules()`, and `ListAlertRules()`.\n\n3. **Main Service**: `pkg/services/ngalert/ngalert.go` contains the `AlertNG` struct which is the primary unified alerting service initialized through `ProvideService()` function. This service orchestrates all alerting components.\n\n4. **Evaluation Engine**: `pkg/services/ngalert/eval/eval.go` implements alert rule evaluation through `NewEvaluatorFactory()` and the `conditionEvaluator` struct, which evaluates alert conditions against datasources.\n\n5. **Scheduler**: `pkg/services/ngalert/schedule/schedule.go` implements the `schedule` struct created by `NewScheduler()`, which periodically evaluates alert rules and manages their execution.\n\n## Migration Script\nThe migration from legacy to unified alerting is implemented across multiple files:\n\n1. **Main Migration**: `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/ualert.go` contains the core migration logic through the `upgradeNgAlerting` struct. The `FixEarlyMigration()` function handles early ng-alerting configurations, while `AddDashboardUIDPanelIDMigration()` migrates dashboard and panel references from annotations. The `upgradeNgAlerting.Exec()` method orchestrates the migration process by updating alert configurations and moving alertmanager files.\n\n2. **Table Creation**: `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/tables.go` defines all new unified alerting tables through `AddTablesMigrations()`, including alert_rule, alert_rule_version, alert_instance, alert_configuration, and related tables.\n\n3. **Migration Service**: `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/migration_service_migration.go` moves legacy alert migration status from the migration log to kvstore.\n\n4. **GUID Migration**: `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/alert_rule_version_guid_mig.go` handles adding and managing GUID columns for alert rules and versions.\n\nThe migration process converts legacy dashboard-based alerts (stored in the `alert` table) into the new unified alert rule format. It preserves metadata through special labels and annotations like `__legacy_` prefixed labels to route alerts through appropriate notification channels and silence rules. The `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/alertrule/compat.go` provides compatibility functions like `convertToK8sResource()` to convert between internal and API formats.\n\nThe unified alerting system is integrated with provisioning services (`pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/alert_rules.go`), state management (`pkg/services/ngalert/state/manager.go`), notification sending (`pkg/services/ngalert/sender/sender.go`), and legacy notification storage (`pkg/services/ngalert/notifier/legacy_storage/compat.go`) to provide backward compatibility while offering a modern alerting platform."
}
